_notes: absorption per ISO 3382-1 / Beranek / Cox & D'Antonio. scattering coefficient per ISO 17497-1 / Cox & D'Antonio (2nd ed.) — reasonable defaults; users can override per zone. Draft engine (Sabine/Hopkins-Stryker/STIPA) ignores scattering; precision ray tracer (Phase B+) uses it for Lambertian vs specular bounce decisions.

materials.name: Heavy carpet on concrete (no underlay)
materials._source: Beranek, Noise & Vibration Control Engineering 2nd ed. table 8.1 (carpet, heavy, glued direct to slab)

materials.name: Heavy carpet on foam underlay
materials._source: Cox & D'Antonio, Acoustic Absorbers and Diffusers 2nd ed. table A.1 (thick pile carpet on foam underlay) — typical residential carpet, much higher absorption above 250 Hz than the bare-glue case

materials.name: Broadband corner bass trap (200mm rockwool)
materials._source: Cox & D'Antonio 2nd ed. table A.4 (corner-mounted 200mm porous, 50mm gap to wall) + Everest & Pohlmann Master Handbook 5th ed. fig 18.7 — broadband, doesn't need tuning

materials.name: Membrane absorber tuned 80 Hz
materials._source: Cox & D'Antonio 2nd ed. §8.4.2 + table 8.3 — narrow-band 5kg/m² panel + 100mm damped cavity, peak 80–100 Hz, secondary peak 250 Hz typical

materials.name: Perforated panel Helmholtz 125 Hz
materials._source: Cox & D'Antonio 2nd ed. §7.3 + ISO 354 archives — 12mm perforated panel 8% open area, 100mm damped cavity, narrow-band 80–160 Hz peak

materials.name: Ceiling cloud 200mm rockwool, 100mm gap
materials._source: Beranek 2nd ed. table 7-3 (200mm porous absorber suspended) — broadband, near-perfect above 250 Hz, useful in bands carpet covers poorly

materials.name: QRD quadratic-residue diffuser (200mm wells)
materials._source: Cox & D'Antonio 2nd ed. §9.3 + ISO 17497-1 — Schroeder QRD, prime 13, well depth 200mm, Lambertian above f0 = c/(2N·d_max). Low absorption, high scattering — preserves energy but redirects it diffusely

materials.name: Perforated metal deck w/ 50mm mineral wool (arena ceiling)
Arena wall — 20% perforated panel / 80% gypsum (typical coverage)
LED video wall panel (flat glass + steel chassis)
Smooth painted plaster on solid backing (premium mall soffit)
